payload.description: _call_claude() in summarize.py uses text=True which cannot handle null bytes in claude output. Fix: use text=False + manual decode with errors=replace. See exophial bug-l2tkm1a.
payload.title: summarize crashes: embedded null byte from subprocess.run text=True
Ingestion does not capture 100% of JSONL session lines in DuckDB
payload.description:
GOAL: every line of every ~/.claude JSONL transcript must become exactly one row in the records table (one row per line, append-only, idempotent, content-faithful). Current ingest_jsonl()/_extract_blobs() in core.py violate this in three ways found during review:

1. MISSING-UUID PK COLLAPSE (silent data loss). core.py:365 uses record.get('uuid', '') and uuid is the records PRIMARY KEY. Every line lacking a uuid maps to the empty-string key; with INSERT OR IGNORE only the FIRST uuid-less record per DB survives and all subsequent uuid-less lines are silently dropped. Fix: synthesize a stable surrogate key for uuid-less lines (e.g. sessionId + line hash or sessionId+offset) instead of '' so each line gets a distinct PK.

2. BLOB OFFLOADING IS UNRECOVERABLE (content not actually in DuckDB). Server always ingests with blob_root set (server.py:147,174). _extract_blobs (core.py:233-297) strips any content block >100KB (BLOB_THRESHOLD_BYTES, blobs.py:24) and replaces it with a '[Large Content: file_id]' marker; the real content goes to the on-disk blob store. read_blob() (blobs.py:67) has ZERO callers, so offloaded content is unreachable through any query/context/summarize path. Net effect: large lines are NOT captured in the queryable store. Fix: either wire read_blob() into the query/context paths to rehydrate, or expose blob content via SQL (e.g. a view/table) so 100% of content is reachable.

3. MALFORMED-LINE + RE-SERIALIZATION CAVEATS. core.py:355 silently skips lines that fail json.loads (acceptable but should be counted/surfaced, not dropped invisibly). Separately, when blob_root is set core.py:361 does line = json.dumps(record) so records.raw is a re-encoded copy, not the original bytes (key order/whitespace/unicode-escaping differ) even for lines with no blob. Verify this is acceptable; if byte-faithfulness is required, store the original line.

Related already-fixed boundary bugs in this same path (is-b3b dropped records under concurrent write/read; is-a2a refresh returning processed:0 for appended lines) show this is the highest-risk area for the 100%-capture invariant. Add a regression test asserting line-count(file) == row-count(records) for a session containing uuid-less lines and >100KB content blocks.

payload.note: Implemented on branch is-a1f-capture-invariant (commit 4599031). 144 tests green incl. live-LLM e2e; pre-commit green. Remaining for close: merge to main, redeploy the running service (uv tool install . + systemctl --user restart ingest-sessions), verify capture_v2 re-ingest against the live DB.

Merged to main (commit 4599031), reinstalled uv tool, restarted systemd service. capture_v2 re-ingest completed in ~13min (checkpoint-bound on a bloated 3.7GB DuckDB file holding only 258MB of live transcripts). Prod-verified via the live MCP query tool on :8741: empty-string PK rows=0, 26940 previously-dropped uuid-less records RECOVERED via surrogate keys, malformed_lines=0, read_blob() SQL round-trip exact (246922 bytes). Service active, NRestarts=0. Pre-migration DB+WAL backed up to ~/.local/share/ingest_sessions/*.bak-pre-isa1f.

payload.description: install.py line 74 hardcodes the Claude Code hook command as: python3 SCRIPT_PATH. Bare system python3 cannot import ingest_sessions because the package lives in the uv-tool ISOLATED venv (~/.local/share/uv/tools/ingest-sessions/...), not system site-packages. So EVERY installed hook (SessionStart session_start + session_retrieve, PreCompact pre_compact, UserPromptSubmit first_prompt_retrieve) fails non-blocking with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ingest_sessions (Traceback on every UserPromptSubmit). Observed on kelce (Ubuntu); system python3.12 present but without the package. FIX: write the command with the installing interpreter, which IS the uv-tool venv python since ingest-sessions-install is a console script in that venv: use sys.executable -m ingest_sessions.hooks.MODULE (module form, no fragile site-packages path), or at minimum sys.executable plus the script path. The python3_available = shutil.which(python3) check at line 171 is the wrong gate: system python3 on PATH does NOT imply it can import the package. Same class as the gateway lint-ratchet env bug (tool resolved off PATH that cannot see the package venv). VERIFIED locally: changing all 4 commands in ~/.claude/settings.json to venv-python -m ingest_sessions.hooks.MODULE resolves the import (session_retrieve injects 1672 chars, first_prompt_retrieve emits hookSpecificOutput, exit 0). Re-running ingest-sessions-install would REINTRODUCE the bug until install.py is fixed. Surfaced while debugging a live UserPromptSubmit hook error in an mtm coordinator session.
payload.title: install.py writes non-portable hook command python3 path; causes ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ingest_sessions

payload.description: Replace the hand-bumped static version (pyproject version = 0.2.0) with a git-derived dynamic version, so the built/installed artifact traces to its build commit (GitOps, matching the gateway version=git_commit model). Static semver already DRIFTED: repo HEAD was several commits past 0.2.0 with no bump (installed 0.2.0 == HEAD only by coincidence). Backend is hatchling, and there are currently ZERO git tags. PRAGMATIC approach chosen by principal (over pure 0.0.0+gSHA): 1) add ONE anchor tag: git tag v0.2.0 on the appropriate commit; 2) pyproject [build-system] requires += hatch-vcs; [project] remove static version, add dynamic = [version]; [tool.hatch.version] source = vcs; 3) versions then become 0.2.0.dev<commits-since-tag>+g<shortsha> — human-readable base AND commit-traceable, PEP 440-valid, never hand-bumped; 4) expose __version__ = importlib.metadata.version(ingest-sessions) in __init__.py (currently absent); 5) reinstall: uv tool install --reinstall <repo>. TARGET DIR: the NESTED project /home/rix/code/git_puller/repos/ingest_sessions/ingest_sessions (holds pyproject.toml + its own .git that hatch-vcs reads). CAVEAT: dynamic versioning needs .git at build time (fine for uv tool install from the repo; hatch-vcs writes _version.py into sdist/wheel for non-git builds). ACCEPTANCE: ingest-sessions --version (or importlib.metadata) reports a git-derived version like 0.2.0.dev3+g<sha>; pyproject no longer carries a static version string; a fresh uv tool install yields a version that traces to the build commit. Pairs with is-dbd (the installer should also write hooks via sys.executable -m, same GitOps/provenance theme).
payload.title: Versioning: switch from static semver to git-derived via hatch-vcs (pragmatic: anchor tag v0.2.0 + dynamic version)
Versioning: replace static semver with pure git-SHA local version (0.0.0+<git-sha>) via hatchling

payload.description: Replace the hand-bumped static version (pyproject version = 0.2.0) with a PURE git-commit version: 0.0.0+<short-git-sha>. NO anchor tag, NO .dev<distance>, NO semver base — the version IS the build commit (GitOps; matches the gateway version=git_commit model). The static semver already drifted (repo HEAD was past 0.2.0 with no bump). IMPLEMENTATION (hatchling-native, no setuptools-scm tag machinery): a custom hatchling version source — hatch_build.py implementing a VersionSourceInterface (or [tool.hatch.version] source pointing at a small function) that returns '0.0.0+g' + (git rev-parse --short HEAD). pyproject: [project] add dynamic = [version], remove the static version; keep build-backend hatchling. Expose __version__ = importlib.metadata.version(ingest-sessions) in __init__.py. Reinstall: uv tool install --reinstall <repo>. WHY drop the tag/.dev<N>+gSHA scheme: that exists only so PyPI packages get monotonic, pip-upgrade-orderable versions — irrelevant for a uv-tool-from-git install, so it's pure overhead. PEP 440 note: 0.0.0+<sha> is valid (the +segment is a local version); it is NOT PyPI-uploadable, which is fine since this is installed from the repo, not published. TARGET DIR: the NESTED project /home/rix/code/git_puller/repos/ingest_sessions/ingest_sessions (pyproject + its .git). CAVEAT: needs .git at build (fine for uv tool install from repo; the version source can fall back to 0.0.0+unknown if .git absent). ACCEPTANCE: ingest-sessions --version (or importlib.metadata) reports 0.0.0+g<sha> matching the build commit; pyproject carries no static version and no tags are involved. Pairs with is-dbd (installer should write hooks via sys.executable -m — same provenance theme).

payload.body: REFINEMENT (principal): version ordering only matters at PyPI publish (an index resolves 'latest'); a uv-tool-from-git install pins the exact ref, so the version is provenance-only. So: SHA-local by default, a real minor number ONLY when publishing. Don't hardcode 0.0.0 permanently — make the custom version source: if HEAD is on an exact tag (git describe --exact-match --tags) emit that tag (e.g. 0.2.0, the PyPI release version); ELSE emit 0.0.0+g<short-sha>. No .dev<N> distance, no semver guessing. Net: every dev/tool build is 0.0.0+g<sha>; publishing to PyPI is just 'git tag 0.2.0' on the release commit (the local +sha is dropped, PyPI gets a clean 0.2.0). This is the whole reconciliation of 'SHA now' + 'minor number for pypi later' in one ~5-line source.

CORRECTION: drop the 'g' prefix — use 0.0.0+<short-sha> (e.g. 0.0.0+1409a31), NOT 0.0.0+g1409a31. The 'g' is just git-describe convention (g=git) with zero functional value; PEP 440 accepts the bare hex local segment fine. Final scheme: tagged commit -> the tag (PyPI minor); else 0.0.0+<short-sha>.

FIXED: _hook_command now returns '<sys.executable> -m ingest_sessions.hooks.<module>' (module form under the installing interpreter = the uv-tool venv python that can import the package), replacing the non-portable 'python3 <path>'. Removed the wrong python3_available = shutil.which('python3') gate (system python3 on PATH != can import the uv-isolated package); check_hooks now reports 'interpreter': sys.executable, and the misleading 'python3 not found' warning is gone. Added regression test test_hook_command_uses_sys_executable_module_form asserting the module form and rejecting the bare 'python3 <path>.py' form. Full suite green (286 passed); pre-commit green. Tokens: ~55k (~$0.4 est).
